rationale: Both candidates miss the four substantive bugs the human reviewers actually flagged (slot leak on abort, cumulative/delta re-slice bug, ceil-division length bug, legacy-tokenizer/v2 config collision), so recall is low for both. X actively asserts 'slot-leak cleanup is handled... lifecycle looks complete,' which is factually wrong and contradicts the ground truth's top High-severity finding — a confident false claim that hurts precision. Y makes no such false assertions, its validated claims track the visible diff accurately, and its findings (max_new_frames crash, ignored sampling params, untested streaming paths) are grounded with specific evidence and concrete literal-code fixes/commands, giving it an edge in both precision and actionability.
